replies.text: Thank you for sharing that detail вАФ local memory and history often carry details that documentaries can easily miss. Scotland has such deep and complex stories, especially around clans, old waters, and places where history still feels alive. Since you lived in Scotland, what part of its history or landscape do you think outsiders misunderstand the most?

comments.text: I have travelled all over Scotland, theyвАЩre hardly hidden,  I swam in this Loch it is absolutely freezing but enjoyed it, why are they saying hidden beauties theyвАЩre far from hidden, we decided to take our two kids from John  oвАЩ grants to lands end, Inverness is nice but there are places people havenвАЩt seen, not that many but itвАЩs all accessible, Wick which is way up north of Scotland рЯПіу†БІу†БҐу†Б≥у†Б£у†Біу†Бњ there are still battlements where the Germans tried to sneak on to win the war, please say the correct words right Scotland рЯПіу†БІу†БҐу†Б≥у†Б£у†Біу†Бњ is our treasure.

comments.author: @GeorgeSergent
comments.text: Another channel destroyed by a stupid/careless remark. Starts out fine (apart from pronunciation!) then ~  Skye ~ "Seabirds such as Albatrosses and Gulls".  The latest (regular/solitary) sighting was in 2024 at Bempton Cliffs, Yorkshire.  A "Black Browed Albatross has returned several times".  His journey is incredible ~ from the Antarctic Seas to Scotland.  An Albatross sighted in Scotland ~ implied on a regular basis? ? ?   This is what sickens me about these videos ~ people say the most absurd things when a simple wee bit of research would make sense.  I now avoid this absurdity.
